name: Claudio Monteverdi
description: Italian composer, string player, choirmaster, and priest (1567–1643)
abstract: Claudio Giovanni Antonio Monteverdi (baptized 15 May 1567 – 29 November 1643) was an Italian composer, choirmaster and string player. A composer of both secular and sacred music, and a pioneer in the development of opera, he is considered a crucial transitional figure between the Renaissance and Baroque periods of music history.

about_quotes.speaker: Dick Spottswood
about_quotes.evidence: I liked classical music. I loved Claudio Monteverdi’s operas and masses
about_quotes.doc_title: Dick Spottswood interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 13, 2023
